text:
Department of Neurology 

Clinic Date: 02 October 2025 

Dr Amelia Carter  
St. Augustine Neurology Centre  
Outpatients, 12 Brookfield Road, Manchester M14 6DN  

Dear Dr Amelia Carter,  

Emily Turner, DOB: 23-02-1992, Hospital No: M937521 NHS No. 6045128739  
Flat 3, 45 Oak Street, Manchester, M1 7AB 

Diagnoses: 

Two unprovoked convulsive episodes (2017, May 2024). MRI brain normal (June 2024). EEG normal (July 2024). 

Seizure Pattern: 

Patient-reported rare episodes temporally linked with high-caffeine intake (e.g., strong coffee/energy drinks) and sleep restriction; no events since June 2024 after moderating caffeine and improving sleep. 

Current Medication: 

Levetiracetam 500 mg twice daily. 

Plan: 

- Continue levetiracetam 500 mg bd; supply repeat prescription for 12 months.  
- Lifestyle reinforcement: maintain reduced caffeine use and regular sleep.  
- Seizure safety advice provided (including driving regulations discussed; patient currently meets advice to notify DVLA if further events occur).  
- No routine follow-up booked; open return via GP if breakthrough events, adverse effects, or pregnancy planning. 

I reviewed Emily Turner today. They report good adherence, no further episodes, and improved alertness after cutting down caffeinated beverages. No adverse effects from levetiracetam. Neurological examination is unremarkable. We discussed risk minimisation and agreed to continue current management. 

With best wishes,

check__Seizure Frequency Number.analysis: Clinic date is 02 Oct 2025. The letter states, "no events since June 2024." Therefore, the patient has had zero seizures from June 2024 up to the clinic date. Without the exact day in June, the seizure-free duration ranges between approximately 15 months (if last event was late June 2024) and 16 months (if early June 2024). This exceeds the 6-month criterion for seizure freedom.

flaws.description: Gold (seizure free for 15 to 16 months) disagrees with Reference (unknown)
